metadata.title: Ending A — The God of Fear & Hunger
metadata.text: Requires the Girl in the party. Destroy all three hearts of the God of the Depths, then descend to the Heart of Darkness. The Girl begins transforming into the God of Fear and Hunger over the course of a boss fight. The protagonist is defeated but acknowledged for their suffering. A voice tells them to lay down and rest so their life essence can usher in something new. The protagonist never left the dungeons, but played a vital part in birthing a new age of the God of Fear and Hunger. This is canonical — the Girl appears as this god in Termina. Any character, any difficulty except the S-endings.

metadata.title: Ending B — The Older One
metadata.text: Same path as Ending A but without the Girl. At the Heart of Darkness, the protagonist faces the Traces of Gro-goroth. Though the boss fight must be won, it is implied the protagonist cannot truly defeat the Old God, who merely stops to ask why they resist. Gro-goroth reveals 'glimpses of the shape everyone shares' — knowledge too vast for any human mind. The protagonist dies from the terror as their bodily functions shut down. Their biggest regret was ever entering the dungeons.

metadata.title: Ending C — The Yellow King
metadata.text: Requires Le'garde alive in the party. Collect the four New God souls (Tormented, Endless, Enlightened, Dominating). Defeat the Nameless for access to the Golden Temple. Kill Francois the Dominant One. At the Throne of Ascension, Le'garde reveals he was faking amnesia — godhood was always his goal. He sits on the throne and teleports away. In the Void, the protagonist finds Le'garde after ~100 years (from his perspective) as the Yellow King. Answer 'You are not my god!' and kill him. Party escapes the dungeons forever scarred.

metadata.title: Ending C-II — The Prophecy
metadata.text: Same path as Ending C, but when Le'garde offers godhood in the Void, kneel before him. He says 'Yes... Love your god.' No final fight needed. Party escapes the dungeons but gained nothing. Years later, rumors spread of the Knights of the Midnight Sun returning. Le'garde unifies the western world, making every right decision as if guided by a higher power. He is throned as the Yellow King, bringing unprecedented prosperity. Decades later, the protagonist glimpses Le'garde — same age but with sickly yellow skin — and concludes he has become something else entirely.

metadata.title: Ending D — A New God
metadata.text: Same path as Ending C but without Le'garde. At the Throne of Ascension, without Le'garde present, the protagonist sits and is transported to the Void where they encounter their own God-like version. The God-like asks how they will rule as a god. Regardless of answers, the playthrough ends with the protagonist ascending to godhood. Answer variants determine the text epilogue describing the protagonist's divine reign.

metadata.title: Ending E — Escape?
metadata.text: Requires finding Le'garde (dead or alive). After discovering his fate, it becomes possible to leave the dungeons from the entrance path at any time. The protagonist and party escape, but remain haunted — their senses indicate they are still within the dungeons. A vision of a Guard manifests. It is left ambiguous whether the protagonist is hallucinating still being in the dungeons, or if the escape itself was the hallucination.

metadata.title: S Endings — Character-Specific Fates
metadata.text: Four S-endings exist, one per character, achievable only on Hard difficulty and not as a Marriage. Cahara's 'Happy Ending': find all three treasures from Isayah's maps in Ma'habre and the King's Crown, then leave the dungeons. Ragnvaldr's ending involves killing Le'garde and escaping with the Cube of the Depths. D'arce's ending involves saving Le'garde. Enki's ending involves ascending through knowledge. Each can only be achieved with the corresponding starting character on Hard mode.

metadata.title: Limb Targeting Combat System
metadata.text: Fear & Hunger's combat revolves around targeting individual enemy limbs. Each limb has its own HP pool. Destroying limbs changes enemy behavior: destroying a cleaver arm removes cleaver attacks, destroying a stinger disables thrust attacks, destroying legs reduces mobility. Enemies can likewise dismember player characters — lost limbs are permanently gone without special restorative items. Strategic limb targeting is essential for survival. Dismemberment is a core part of combat for all characters.

metadata.title: Coin Flip Mechanic
metadata.text: Many enemies have a coin flip attack — an unavoidable instant-kill or massive-damage move on specific turns. Players must Guard on the coin flip turn to survive. Destroying the limb that enables the coin flip disables it (e.g., the Guard's right arm for Mad Rush). Coin flips have a 50% success rate. Understanding enemy coin flip patterns is critical — failure often means death. Coin flip turns follow predictable patterns (every 3 turns for Guards).

metadata.title: Saving and Permanent Death
metadata.text: The game can only be saved at ritual circles scattered throughout the dungeons. There is no automatic checkpointing. Death is permanent. Players must weigh the risk of pressing forward against the safety of backtracking to save. Some ritual circles are hidden or require specific conditions to access. This creates genuine tension — every decision carries weight.

metadata.title: No Leveling System — Stats
metadata.text: Fear & Hunger has no experience points or leveling. Character stats improve only through equipment, consumable items (Blue Vials for attack), or by interacting with entities like the Human Hydra with a Ring of Wraiths. This makes base stats critical and equipment choices life-or-death. Knowledge of the game's mechanics and world is the player's main leverage — the game rewards learning through failure.

metadata.title: Soul Types and Ascension
metadata.text: Characters possess distinct soul types with narrative significance: Cahara has the Endless soul (yearning for freedom), D'arce has the Domination soul (natural leadership), Enki has the Enlightened soul (pursuit of knowledge), Ragnvaldr has the Tormented soul (forged by suffering), the Girl has the Ancient One soul (divine potential). The New Gods each embody one soul type. Soul stones can be used on corpses to acquire souls. Le'garde's plan involves encompassing all soul types to transcend the limits of any single New God.

metadata.title: Hunger and Survival Mechanics
metadata.text: Hunger is a persistent mechanic — characters must eat food items or suffer increasing penalties. Starvation reduces stats and eventually kills. Food is scavenged from the environment, dropped by enemies, or bought from merchants. Some food can cause infection or poisoning. Mind represents mental stability — low mind triggers phobias, hallucinations, and combat penalties. Mind is restored through items like Tobacco, reading books, or resting in safe areas. Infection worsens over time if untreated and must be cured with Green Herbs.

metadata.title: Marriage and Fusion
metadata.text: At ritual circles, two party members can be merged into a Marriage — a powerful combined entity. A Marriage (Fusion) is a further evolution. Marriages cannot achieve character-specific S-endings. This is a permanent and irreversible choice. The Abominable Marriage is a variant with different implications. Buckman's party (Ser Seymor and companions) form a failed marriage in the Level 7 Catacombs if spoken to, marking the end of their journey.

metadata.title: Fear & Hunger — World Overview
metadata.text: Fear & Hunger is a horror dungeon crawler set in the dark dungeons of fear and hunger beneath the Kingdom of Rondon, released December 2018. Four adventurers — Cahara (mercenary), D'arce (knight), Enki (dark priest), and Ragnvaldr (outlander) — delve into the depths, each with their own motives. The fortress acts as an ancient nexus for different planes of existence where old and new gods struggle for power. The game features lovecraftian horror elements, limb-targeting combat, and permanent consequences. The ancient city of Ma'habre lies beneath the dungeons, once home to the Old Gods and later the New Gods, now decaying and largely forgotten.

metadata.title: The Prophecy of Le'garde
metadata.text: Le'garde is a prophesied leader and captain of the Knights of the Midnight Sun, a mercenary group. Ancient prophecies foretold a figure who would unify the western world without violence. Le'garde's magnetic leadership drew followers including D'arce Cataliss. His success was attributed to a prophetic vision of a unified kingdom. Over time, the New God Nilvan came to believe Le'garde held the key to her eternal existence, visiting him in a dream and conceiving the Girl. Le'garde became obsessed with ancient texts and the occult, invading Oldegård for the Cube of the Depths. This aggression allowed Rondon to imprison him in the dungeons where Trortur tortured him.

metadata.title: Gods of Fear & Hunger
metadata.text: The pantheon includes Old Gods (Gro-goroth, Sylvian, God of the Depths, Rher, Vinushka) who created the world and have since departed. New Gods are former mortals who ascended using the secrets of Ma'habre: The Fellowship consists of Valteil (Enlightenment), Nilvan (Endless), Francóis (Domination), and the Tormented One. Ascended Gods include Alll-mer and the God of Fear and Hunger. Each New God embodies a distinct soul type that grants their power. Le'garde's plot involves encompassing all soul aspects (torment through torture, enlightenment through the Cube, endlessness through Nilvan, domination through his campaign) to become greater than any New God.

metadata.title: Ma'habre — The Ancient City of the Gods
metadata.text: Ma'habre (also Ma'havre) is the ancient city of the gods beneath the Dungeons of Fear and Hunger. Originally built to worship the Old Gods, it later became home to the New Gods who transformed it according to their whims. The grand libraries were created by Betel, a New God of Enlightenment. In the year 809, the Fellowship descended into the city and became New Gods themselves. By 1590, Ma'habre is decaying and forgotten, with the aged New Gods having slowly lost their powers. The Cube of the Depths is required to access Ma'habre and can also be used as a Beacon of the Depths to travel between the past (thriving Ma'habre) and present (dilapidated Ancient City).

metadata.title: The Four Protagonists — Motivations
metadata.text: Cahara is a mercenary from the Eastern Sanctuaries hired by Rondon to extract Le'garde — he seeks money to free his lover Celeste, a prostitute in indentured servitude. D'arce is a Rondon knight who left the kingdom's army disillusioned and joined the Knights of the Midnight Sun — she loves Le'garde and enters the dungeons to save him. Enki is a dark priest driven by his enlightened soul to pursue forbidden knowledge — he seeks the secrets of the dungeons and Ma'habre. Ragnvaldr is an outlander from Oldegård whose tribe was massacred by the Knights of the Midnight Sun — he seeks vengeance on Le'garde and to recover the Cube of the Depths stolen from his people.

metadata.title: The Throne of Ascension
metadata.text: The Throne of Ascension is located in the Golden Temple within Ma'habre. It is the mechanism by which mortals can achieve godhood. To access it, one must collect the four souls of the New Gods (Tormented, Endless, Enlightened, and Dominating) and defeat the Nameless guardian. Sitting on the throne transports the user to The Void where the final confrontation occurs. This is Le'garde's ultimate goal — to seize power greater than any New God by encompassing all aspects of divinity. Nosramus, the ancient alchemist, describes Le'garde's plan as not necessarily the right path but an interesting event to unfold.

metadata.title: Dungeons of Fear & Hunger — Structure
metadata.text: The Dungeons of Fear and Hunger are an ancient fortress beneath the Kingdom of Rondon. The structure spans multiple levels: Level 1 Entrance, Level 2 Basement, Level 3 Prisons / Inner Hall, Level 4 Courtyard, Level 5 Mines, Level 6 Ancient City outskirts, and Level 7 Catacombs. Beneath all is the ancient city of Ma'habre, accessible through the Catacombs near Le'garde's cell. The dungeons feature ritual circles for saving, randomized loot and enemy placements, and environmental storytelling through notes and environmental details. Maps are interconnected through staircases and hidden passages.

metadata.title: Entrance — Level 1
metadata.text: The Entrance is the starting point. The player begins here after descending into the dungeons. Basic items are available: torches, a Tinderbox/Lighter, and healing herbs. The area connects to the Basement (Level 2). Early enemies include Guards and Maneba (Jagged Jaw). This is a tutorial zone where players learn limb-targeting mechanics and resource management. A ritual circle may be found here.

metadata.title: Prisons — Level 3
metadata.text: The Prisons contain cells, torture rooms, and hostile Guards. Key NPCs: Buckman (fleeing Trortur), Trortur (the torturer). The Girl is found in a cage here. Elite Guards patrol this area. A ritual circle exists in a safer room. This area contains lore through Torturer's Notes and the Diary of an Unknown Guard. The player can choose to kill Trortur for Buckman's quest or betray Buckman to Trortur.

metadata.title: Courtyard — Level 4
metadata.text: The Courtyard is an open area featuring statues, a well, and multiple connections. It serves as a crossroads linking the Entrance, Prisons, Library, and Mines. Guard Ballistas and Cave Gnomes patrol here. Notable NPCs may pass through. The Courtyard is a critical junction point for navigation and strategic planning.

metadata.title: Mines — Level 5
metadata.text: The Mines are extremely dark — torches are essential. Cave Gnomes are the primary enemy. The area contains mining equipment, dangerous pits, valuable ore, and crafting materials. Falling into pits can lead to secret areas or instant death. The Mines connect to deeper dungeon sections. Near the mines is the Cavedweller village on Level 6, and the path to the God of the Depths' mouth.

metadata.title: Catacombs — Level 7
metadata.text: The Catacombs are the deepest level before Ma'habre. Le'garde is found imprisoned here in a cell. The Cube of the Depths is needed to access Ma'habre nearby. The Catacombs contain dangerous enemies. This is a critical story junction — whether Le'garde is dead or alive determines available endings. Buckman's party (Ser Seymor and possibly Jeanne) may be found in the basement area of this level.

metadata.title: Ma'habre — Key Locations
metadata.text: Past Ma'habre (thriving): Grand Library (Betel's knowledge), Temple of Torment (Tormented One), Tower of Endless (Nilvan), Temple District (Valteil), Golden Temple (Francois and the Throne of Ascension). Center Square and Downtown connect areas. Present Ma'habre (Ancient City, dilapidated): same layout but decayed, overrun by nature, with different enemies (Cave spiders, Lord of Flies, Scarabs, Uterus). The Cube of the Depths serves as a Beacon to switch between past and present at obelisk devices. Key NPCs in Ma'habre: Pocketcat, Isayah (map merchant), Bug-Eyed Figurine, Ancient Figurine, Old Guardian, Nameless.

metadata.title: The Girl — Origins and Role
metadata.text: The Girl is a nameless, mute child imprisoned within the dungeons. She is the daughter of the New God Nilvan and Le'garde, conceived in a dream when Nilvan visited Le'garde hoping to create a being that would transcend humanity. The Girl bears an Ancient One soul, a dormant spark of divine potential. She appears deeply traumatized, unused to kindness. She is found in a cage early in the dungeons and can be recruited as a party member. She is very weak in combat but will sacrifice herself to protect party members below 25% HP. She can be given a Peculiar Doll and a Dagger, after which she speaks her only line: 'y-yes.' She can be sold to Pocketcat or sacrificed at ritual circles.

metadata.title: The Girl — Fate and Ascension
metadata.text: If the Girl is brought to the Altar of Darkness within the body of the God of the Depths, she begins the process of ascension to divinity, shedding her human skin and becoming the God of Fear and Hunger. This requires destroying the three hearts of the God of the Depths and descending to the Heart of Darkness. Her ascension is canonical — she appears in Fear & Hunger 2: Termina as a revered deity. This is Ending A. Nilvan hoped her daughter would become a beacon of hope for mankind, transcending her own limited lifespan as a New God.

metadata.title: Le'garde — The Prophesied One
metadata.text: Le'garde is the captain of the Knights of the Midnight Sun and the central figure driving the four protagonists into the dungeons. He is a charismatic leader who believes in unifying the kingdom through non-violent means, but paradoxically was willing to invade Oldegård and kill to obtain the Cube of the Depths. He was captured by Rondon and imprisoned in the dungeons where Trortur tortured him. His amnesia after being rescued is a facade — he manipulates his savior into helping him reach ascension. Each aspect of his suffering was orchestrated: torment through torture, enlightenment through the Cube, endlessness through Nilvan, and domination through his military campaign.

metadata.title: Le'garde — Fate and Endings
metadata.text: Le'garde's survival determines several endings. If found alive and kept in the party, he can ascend via the Throne of Ascension. In Ending C (The Yellow King), the protagonist rejects his godhood and kills him — party escapes, scarred forever. In Ending C-II (The Prophecy), the protagonist kneels — Le'garde unifies the western world and brings prosperity as the Yellow King. In Ending E, finding Le'garde dead or alive allows escape from the dungeons. Le'garde's future self exists in Termina as Kaiser. If Ragnvaldr is in the party, a confrontation occurs between them.

metadata.title: Buckman — The Crown Prince
metadata.text: Lord Ludwig Buckman is the Crown Prince of the Kingdom of Rondon. He led a party consisting of Ser Seymor, Jeanne, and Ser Seril into the Dungeons of Fear and Hunger to investigate what happened to Captain Rudimer. The party was ambushed and dispersed soon after entering. Buckman is found alone on Level 1 Inner Hall, having escaped from the torturer Trortur. He is panicked and advises the player that stealth is the only way to survive. He has lost his sense of time and does not know if he has been trapped for weeks, months, or years.

metadata.title: Buckman — Questline
metadata.text: Buckman's quest involves reuniting him with his three companions. Killing Trortur and informing Buckman triggers his move to Level 2 basement to reunite with Ser Seymor. This must be done before visiting Ma'habre or Buckman is killed by Trortur — his body found cut in half on a torture table. Jeanne is found in Ma'habre being attacked by a Lord of Flies — rescuing her returns her to the basement. Ser Seril is found as an enemy in past Ma'habre — using a Potion of Mind on him in combat restores his sanity and he reunites. After all four are reunited, they leave the dungeons, leaving Buckman's letter and a Ring of Wraiths as a gift. Buckman asks the player to end Captain Rudimer's suffering if found.

metadata.title: Cahara — The Mercenary
metadata.text: Cahara is a playable character from Jettaiah in the Eastern Sanctuaries. Born with the soul of the endless, he was abandoned at birth and grew up choosing between crime and honest trade. He joined mercenary bands and eventually a notorious highwayman's gang, raiding across the lands. In Rondon, he fell in love with Celeste, a prostitute in indentured servitude, and seeks money to buy her freedom. Rondon officials hired him to extract Le'garde from the dungeons. Cahara is known for dirty tactics, craftiness, and doing whatever brings silver. He has no phobias.

metadata.title: D'arce — The Knight
metadata.text: D'arce Cataliss is a playable character from the Kingdom of Rondon. Born with the soul of domination, she was raised in nobility and became a holy knight of Alll-mer. Disillusioned by Rondon's pursuit of power benefiting only the elite, she left the army and joined the Knights of the Midnight Sun. She fell deeply in love with Le'garde, though he viewed her only as a valuable subordinate. She enters the dungeons to rescue him. D'arce is a formidable warrior excelling in close combat with swords and heavy armor. She has no phobias.

metadata.title: Enki — The Dark Priest
metadata.text: Enki Ankarian is a playable character, a dark priest from a lineage with centuries-long occult connections. Born with the soul of the enlightened, he pursued forbidden knowledge endlessly. During his initiation, he was forced to fight his twin sister with ceremonial knives — she spared him. Playing as Enki, the player can choose to kill her anyway to appease the high priests, or accept defeat and be cast into a well where he learns the language of insects. He enters the dungeons seeking ancient knowledge and the secrets of Ma'habre. He bears no burden of morality or ethics and has edge in blood magic, but his devotion to magic left him physically weak.

metadata.title: Ragnvaldr — The Outlander
metadata.text: Ragnvaldr is a playable character from the northern Kingdom of Oldegård. Born with the soul of the tormented, he was destined to face relentless struggle, which forged his strength and iron will. He sailed to the dark continent of Vinland and survived, bringing back the Cube of the Depths. The Knights of the Midnight Sun invaded Oldegård to steal the Cube, massacring his tribe and killing his wife Hilde and son Björn. He enters the dungeons seeking vengeance on Le'garde and to recover the Cube. Ragnvaldr is an expert hunter and survivalist with proficiency in bows and axes. He has no phobias.

metadata.title: Trortur — The Torturer
metadata.text: Trortur is the insane torturer of the dungeons. He tortured Le'garde and is responsible for tormenting prisoners in the dungeon's lower levels. Buckman escaped from him. The player can encounter Trortur in the prisons. Killing him is part of Buckman's quest — if he is not killed before the player visits Ma'habre, Buckman is found dead on a torture table, cut in half. The player can also betray Buckman to Trortur in exchange for a Level 2F key and two Light Blue Vials.

metadata.title: Nosramus — The Ancient Alchemist
metadata.text: Nosramus is an ancient alchemist residing deep in the dungeons. He is knowledgeable about the dungeon's secrets, alchemical recipes, and the history of Ma'habre. He describes Le'garde's plan to ascend as 'not necessarily the right path, but an interesting event to unfold.' Nosramus provides crucial lore about the New Gods' souls and the Throne of Ascension. He is one of the few genuinely helpful NPCs in the dungeons.

metadata.title: Pocketcat — The Sinister Merchant
metadata.text: Pocketcat is a mysterious merchant NPC who appears in the dungeons and Ma'habre. He offers unique items and can take the Girl from the party if the player agrees to sell her. If the player refuses twice, Pocketcat appears at the Golden Temple after the Nameless Figurine is defeated — refusing a third time causes him to give up and disappear forever. Accepting results in the Girl being permanently removed. Pocketcat speaks in riddles and his true nature is ambiguous.
